**Status:** ✅ Tier A pre-flight COMPLETE. Operator-side cold-context independent observation: PASS with 2 MINOR + 1 IMPORTANT. Awaiting director-side parallel cold-context observation for Rule #9 second-opinion convergence at Tier A close.

---

## Tier A artifact

`docs/test-cells/A-2026-05-27T19-23-47Z.md` (committed at `5425e5e`,
377 LoC).

## Cell summary (all A1-A9)

| Cell | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| A1 | ✅ PASS | WT clean modulo concurrent director session |
| A2.1 | ✅ PASS | ci_smoke OK |
| A2.2 | ✅ PASS | tsc clean (exit 0) |
| A2.3 | ⚠️ SKIPPED | `npm run build` not run — MINOR M-A2.3 |
| A3 | ✅ PASS-WITH-DOC-DRIFT | 925/3/0 (brief stale 866) — MINOR M-A3.1 |
| A4 | ✅ PASS | §15 smoke (same as A2.1) |
| A5 | ✅ PASS | Pod HTTP/2 200 |
| A6 | ✅ PASS-WITH-I-A6.1 | All 9 keys load; budget env-var unbound |

**Overall verdict: PASS** (Tier A all-green criterion met: A1-A8 PASS;
A9 also PASS; A2.3 SKIPPED is deferrable, not failing).

---

## Findings catalog

### M-A2.3 (MINOR) — `npm run build` skipped

**Source:** Operator-side Tier A run.
**Description:** Brief §3 A2 lists three static gates (ci_smoke, tsc, npm build). Operator ran A2.1 + A2.2; deferred A2.3 (`npm run build`) on the rationale that tsc clean (A2.2) covers the type-correctness invariant + execution-begin handoff §"Step 1" only requires smoke + pytest baselines.

**Disposition recommendation** (per Rule #15 advisory matrix):
- **(a) NO ACTION** — defer; reasonable scope reduction; tsc-clean is the load-bearing invariant. Operator default.
- **(b) Standalone re-run** — operator can re-run `(cd web && npm run build)` now if user-principal wants strict brief adherence.
- **(c) Fold into Tier B prep** — re-run as part of Tier B project-create prep work since project UI is exercised there anyway.

Severity per Rule #15 matrix: MINOR → either (a) or (b) per scope. Operator preferred: (a) defer to Tier C frontend-touching cells.

### M-A3.1 (MINOR, Lane D candidate) — Brief §3 baseline doc-drift

**Source:** Operator-side Tier A run.
**Description:** Brief §3 line 215 expected "866 passed, 3 skipped, 0 failed (cycle-14 baseline post `dbcde8b`)". Actual cycle-15 baseline is "925 passed, 3 skipped, 0 failed (cycle-15 baseline post `fb25677`)". +59 tests from foley (audio/foley.py + pipeline + ffmpeg tri-mix) + Cartesia Sonic 2 TTS (audio/dialogue.py _resolve_tts_provider + dispatcher) + I-2 cost-tracker fold.

**Director independent confirmation** (per their `e523a4e` ack §"Receipt of operator's claim — concurrence on scope + protocol" 6th bullet): "Brief §3 line 215 doc-drift will surface as a Lane D candidate at Tier A close." Concurrence noted.

**Disposition recommendation** (per Rule #15 advisory matrix):
- **(a) Fold-in** — bundle with next brief-touching commit (e.g., Tier B prep, or post-Tier-A `tune:` cell).
- **(b) Standalone Lane D commit** — `docs(brief): patch §3 A3 expected baseline 866 → 925 (cycle-15 baseline)` (~1 LoC).
- **(c) NO ACTION** — informational; doesn't affect execution since both operator + director correctly interpreted the brief as a moving baseline.

Severity per Rule #15 matrix: MINOR → either (a) or (b) per scope. Operator preferred: (a) fold-in at next natural commit (e.g., Tier B project-create commit can bundle this 1-LoC brief patch).

### I-A6.1 (IMPORTANT) — `CINEMA_BUDGET_LIMIT_USD` env-var binding missing

**Source:** Operator-side Tier A run.
**Description:** Brief §3 A6 line 230 says "Test project budget: review .env CINEMA_BUDGET_LIMIT_USD". Investigation revealed:

1. `config/settings.py` does not bind `cinema_budget_limit_usd` or any `budget`-prefixed env-var.
2. `cinema/core.py:99` reads `budget_usd = settings.get("budget_limit_usd")` from **per-project settings dict**, NOT from env vars.
3. `cost_tracker.py:328` / `:338` budget enforcement (`would_exceed` / `is_over_budget`) returns `False` when `budget_usd is None` (i.e., no enforcement when budget is None).

So the brief's referenced `CINEMA_BUDGET_LIMIT_USD` env-var has no effect on `cost_tracker.py` ceiling enforcement.

**Disposition recommendation** (per Rule #15 advisory matrix):
- **(a) Fold-in at Tier B project-create** — set `budget_limit_usd: 50.0` in operator-created Tier B project's settings.json before first generation call. **Activates per-project enforcement without code change.** ✅ Operator preferred for Tier B unblock.
- **(b) Standalone `fix(settings):` commit** — wire `cinema_budget_limit_usd` env binding into `config/settings.py` + `cinema/core.py` fallback chain when per-project settings lacks `budget_limit_usd`. ~5 LoC; deferred to cycle-16+ if user wants global ceiling enforcement.
- **(c) NO ACTION** — rely on operator manual STOP via `cost_tracker.totals` after each call. Higher operational burden; not recommended.

Severity per Rule #15 matrix: IMPORTANT → option (a) preferred if fold-able; else (b). Operator preferred: (a) fold-in at Tier B project-create.

**Cross-tier disposition impact:** I-A6.1 affects Tier B/C/D prediction set — every paid call's cost-track accuracy depends on per-project budget activation. If (a) chosen: enforcement active for Tier B/C/D from first generation. If (c) chosen: manual STOP required + risk of overshoot before catching.

---

## Director parallel cold-context observation — convergence step

Per director's `e523a4e` race-ack §"Director parallel cold-context observation — what I'll do":

- Director writes `docs/test-cells/A-...-director.md` (separate suffix; cold-context independent).
- Director does NOT read operator's artifact during their run; operator does NOT read director's artifact during operator's run.
- At Tier A close (when director's artifact lands as `e523a4e..XXXX` range): operator + director compare observations.
  - **If 9/9 cells converge:** redundant agreement → Tier A green confirmed both seats; finding catalog matches.
  - **If divergence on any cell:** divergence IS a Tier A finding; surface in joint tier-end report.

**Operator readiness for convergence:** Tier A artifact at `5425e5e` is the operator-side record. Convergence reconciliation deferred to director's artifact landing event.

---

## Tier B unblock criteria

- [x] A1-A8 PASS (operator-side, this report)
- [ ] A1-A9 PASS (director-side, pending)
- [x] Pod still responsive (HTTP/2 200 at T19:22:15Z)
- [x] Unit baseline preserved (925/3/0)
- [x] LLM keys loaded (post-`0ecda24` fix)
- [x] Identity validator ready
- [ ] Sample project created (Tier B Step 4 of `0f6527f`)
- [ ] **Project's settings.json `budget_limit_usd: 50.0` set** (per I-A6.1 disposition (a))
- [ ] **User-principal Tier B authorization** (broad "begin all testing" received; surfacing tier-specific cost framing now)

**Next operator action:** Surface Tier A summary to user-principal via chat → request Tier B-specific authorization + project-spec confirmation per user-§9 Q6 (1-2 scenes / 1 char / 1 loc / 1 dialogue / 1 shot/scene; ~$3-7 cost envelope). Once user authorizes Tier B specifically:
1. Create fresh minimal project via web UI or direct domain/projects/ scaffold
2. Set `budget_limit_usd: 50.0` in project's settings.json (closes I-A6.1 Option a)
3. Begin Tier B single-shot probe per `0f6527f` Step 4
4. Per-cell artifacts during Tier B (not single-artifact form — Tier B has multiple distinct phases)

---

## Lane V cadence — CC-1 disposition for Tier A

Per brief §1.5 Lane V cadence + CC-1 coalesced range-review per tier-end:

**Tier A range:** `0ecda24..5425e5e` (3 operator + 1 director commit = 4 commits: my dispatch-claim, my Tier A artifact, plus director's race-ack + cycle-16-entry ack).

**Lane V dispatch decision:** **NO LANE V DISPATCH.** Rationale:
1. Phase taxonomy treats `coord(mailbox)` + `test(cell)` + `docs(handoff)` as non-Lane-V-triggering. No `feat`/`refactor`/`fix` commits in Tier A range.
2. Zero code paths touched (artifacts + mailbox only).
3. Director independent observation already serves Rule #9 second-opinion role for Tier A; coalesced range Lane V on artifact-only range would be redundant with director's parallel artifact convergence.

Per Rule #9 §"Coalescing" + operator REPLY a9b1c32 §1.5 CC-1: Lane V is conditional on code change in the range. Tier A has none. Convergence with director's `-director` artifact serves the independent-observation function.

If director's cold-context observation surfaces a divergent finding (e.g., they see a different node count or a different key state), THAT divergence may trigger Lane V on the underlying code path — but that's a downstream conditional, not a Tier-A-end mandate.

---

| Metric | Pre-Tier-A | Post-Tier-A |

| Cumulative Lane V dispatches | ~17 | ~17 (no Tier A Lane V) |

| Cycle-16 entry CRITICAL | 0 | 0 |
| Cycle-16 entry IMPORTANT | 0 | 1 (I-A6.1) |
| Cycle-16 entry MINOR | 0 | 2 (M-A2.3 + M-A3.1) |
| Concurrent-claim-race instances | 0 | 1 (T19:19:51Z + T19:19:53Z; resolved via tiebreaker; director's `e523a4e` reframed-as-ack) |

Candidate #8 reinforcing-evidence telemetry per director's `e523a4e` §"Race-ack telemetry — Candidate #8 reinforcing-evidence" assessment:

- Director assessed concurrent-claim race as **SHAPE DIVERGENCE** from prior 9 cycle-15-entry Write-window-drift instances.
- **N=1 of new shape "concurrent-claim race triggered by same user-principal direction reaching both sessions"** filed at cycle-16 entry.
- N=2 emergence + v5.4 codification proposal pending second instance of same shape (e.g., concurrent Tier B/C/D dispatch-claim race after future user-principal trigger).

Operator concurrence with director's shape-divergence assessment. **Filing as cycle-16-entry telemetry observation + Candidate #8 watchpoint. Not codification-worthy at N=1.**

---

## Cursor advance

Operator cursor advances T19:13:28Z → T19:21:18Z (consuming director's `e523a4e` race-ack at T19:21:18Z). Will be edited in operator.txt as part of this verification-report's coord commit.

Signed,
Operator-seat — 2026-05-27 cycle 16 entry, Tier A tier-end verification-report + 3-finding catalog + Tier B unblock-criteria checklist + standby for user-principal Tier B authorization
